description: Vertically stacked, expandable panels. Each AccordionTrigger is a real <button> with aria-expanded and aria-controls wired to its AccordionContent region. Single-open and multi-open behaviors are supported via the type prop; Radix keeps aria-expanded in sync with visibility either way.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to the next trigger (or out of the accordion).
Moves focus to the next trigger; wraps to first at the end.
Moves focus to the previous trigger; wraps to last at the start.
Moves focus to the first trigger.
Moves focus to the last trigger.

focus.notes: Panels and their contents share the same tab sequence as the rest of the page; focus is never trapped inside an open panel.

announcements: Each trigger is announced as a button with its current expanded state.
Content regions are announced as "region" labelled by their trigger.
screenReader.notes: If you replace AccordionTrigger with a custom element via asChild, keep it a <button> so the implicit role survives; non-button triggers must add role="button" and tabIndex={0}.

contrast.text: Trigger label uses foreground; meets WCAG AA on the default theme
contrast.ui: Chevron icon uses muted-foreground; still meets WCAG AA on bg-background

motion.reducedMotion: Expand / collapse uses a CSS height transition driven by Radix's data-state attributes. Consumers can gate the transition behind @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to skip the animation.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a clear, descriptive label for each AccordionTrigger; it's the only text a screen reader has to convey what's inside.
When embedding interactive content inside a panel, ensure its tab order flows naturally after the trigger.
